page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration consists of 4 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz with 2 threads per core and a 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 923.27 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 84.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server demonstrates average performance in database operations, static web serving at 7.47 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 13.03 million operations per second. However, LLM inference on larger models is a bottleneck, with prompt processing on a 7B model scoring in the bottom 25%.

The server offers a high-density compute profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require substantial processing power and fast local storage without the overhead of dedicated CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under noisy-neighbor conditions, alongside the relatively low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core.

This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, intermediate database operations, and lightweight background processing tasks. It is also appropriate for software compilation and basic text or image processing workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (4 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a compute-leaning resource balance, though the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff in performance consistency. This server is well-suited for static web serving, Redis caching, moderate database workloads, and lightweight multi-threaded applications that benefit from fast local NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU resources or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for web serving and database workloads.

tagline: A compute-leaning virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with fast local NVMe storage for balanced web and database workloads.

350 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
